graph_navigation.title: Getting Around
graph_navigation.content:
Navigate your audio graph like a pro:

🖱️ Pan - Drag on empty space to move the view
🔍 Zoom - Scroll wheel or +/- keys
🎯 Reset - Press Ctrl+0 to fit everything in view

The graph can be as big as you need - just pan and zoom to explore!
graph_navigation.tip: Lost in a big graph? Ctrl+0 instantly shows you everything.

selecting_nodes.title: Selecting Nodes
selecting_nodes.content:
Master selection and you'll master Pipeflow:

• Click - Select one node
• Shift+Click - Add to selection
• Ctrl+Click - Toggle in selection
• Box Select - Draw a rectangle to select multiple
• Ctrl+A - Select all visible nodes
selecting_nodes.tip: Selected nodes show their details in the Inspector panel (press I).

smart_reorganize.title: Smart Reorganize ✨
smart_reorganize.content:
Press Ctrl+Shift+R for automatic layout magic! Smart Reorganize:

• Arranges nodes in a logical flow (inputs → outputs)
• Minimizes connection crossings
• Spaces nodes evenly
• Keeps groups together
• Moves 'uninteresting' nodes to the edges
smart_reorganize.tip: New to a messy graph? Smart Reorganize is your first step to clarity.

snap_to_grid.title: Snap to Grid
snap_to_grid.content:
Enable Snap to Grid for clean, aligned layouts. When enabled:

• Nodes align to an invisible grid when moved
• Everything lines up nicely
• Your graph looks organized and professional

Find the toggle in the toolbar or use the context menu.
snap_to_grid.tip: Snap to Grid + Smart Reorganize = instant organization!

spacing_controls.title: Adjusting Spacing
spacing_controls.content:
Control how spread out your nodes are:

➕ Increase Spacing - Push nodes further apart
➖ Decrease Spacing - Bring nodes closer together
🎯 Compact to Center - Pull everything toward the middle

Find these in the View menu or toolbar.
spacing_controls.tip: More spacing makes connections easier to follow; less spacing fits more on screen.

uninteresting_nodes.title: Marking Nodes as Uninteresting
uninteresting_nodes.content:
Some nodes you just don't care about (system internals, rarely-used devices). Mark them as 'uninteresting' and:

• They'll be moved to the edges during reorganization
• They'll have reduced visual prominence
• They won't clutter your main working area

Right-click a node and choose 'Mark as Uninteresting'.
uninteresting_nodes.tip: Monitors, dummy sinks, and PipeWire internals are good candidates for 'uninteresting'.

context_menus.title: Context Menus
context_menus.content:
Right-click for context-sensitive options:

On empty space:
• View options, layout controls, paste

On a node:
• Node actions, grouping, interesting toggle

On a link:
• Remove connection, toggle enabled state
context_menus.tip: When in doubt, right-click! Most actions are just a click away.
